quiz.title: Unit 6 Quiz 4, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Balancing Equations with Coefficients
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Balancing Chemical Equations, Decomposition Reactions, Single Replacement Reactions, Determining Correct Coefficients, Law of Conservation of Mass

questions.question: Magnesium reacts with hydrochloric acid. The balanced equation is:
questions.explanation: Magnesium reacts with hydrochloric acid to produce magnesium chloride and hydrogen gas. Magnesium forms Mg²⁺, so it needs two chloride ions, requiring two HCl molecules: $\text{Mg} + 2\text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{MgCl}_2 + \text{H}_2$.

questions.question: Ammonium chloride decomposes upon heating. The balanced equation is:
questions.explanation: Ammonium chloride decomposes upon heating to form ammonia gas and hydrogen chloride gas. This is a reversible reaction where the solid sublimes and dissociates: $\text{NH}_4\text{Cl} \rightarrow \text{NH}_3 + \text{HCl}$.

questions.question: Sodium bicarbonate decomposes upon heating. The balanced equation is:
questions.explanation: Sodium bicarbonate (baking soda) decomposes when heated to form sodium carbonate, carbon dioxide, and water: $2\text{NaHCO}_3 \rightarrow \text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3 + \text{CO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$.

questions.question: Hydrochloric acid reacts with zinc. The balanced equation is:
questions.explanation: Zinc reacts with hydrochloric acid to produce zinc chloride and hydrogen gas. Zinc forms Zn²⁺, so it needs two chloride ions, requiring two HCl molecules: $\text{Zn} + 2\text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{ZnCl}_2 + \text{H}_2$.

questions.question: Potassium reacts with water. The balanced equation is:
questions.explanation: Potassium reacts vigorously with water to produce potassium hydroxide and hydrogen gas. Like other alkali metals, the balanced equation is $2\text{K} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{KOH} + \text{H}_2$.

questions.question: Hydrogen peroxide decomposes. The balanced equation is:
questions.explanation: Hydrogen peroxide decomposes into water and oxygen gas. This decomposition is often catalyzed by light or manganese dioxide: $2\text{H}_2\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{H}_2\text{O} + \text{O}_2$.

questions.question: Iron reacts with sulfur. The balanced equation is:
questions.explanation: Iron reacts with sulfur when heated to form iron(II) sulfide. Iron forms Fe²⁺ and sulfur forms S²⁻, so they combine in a 1:1 ratio: $\text{Fe} + \text{S} \rightarrow \text{FeS}$.

questions.question: Silver nitrate reacts with hydrochloric acid. The balanced equation is:
questions.explanation: Silver nitrate reacts with hydrochloric acid to form silver chloride (a white precipitate) and nitric acid. This is a double replacement reaction: $\text{AgNO}_3 + \text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{AgCl} + \text{HNO}_3$.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: ____$\text{C}_3\text{H}_8$ + ____$\text{O}_2$ → ____$\text{CO}_2$ + ____$\text{H}_2\text{O}$
questions.explanation: Propane combustion: $\text{C}_3\text{H}_8 + 5\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 3\text{CO}_2 + 4\text{H}_2\text{O}$. This gives 3 C, 8 H, and 10 O on both sides.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: ____$\text{Fe}$ + ____$\text{O}_2$ → ____$\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3$
questions.explanation: $4\text{Fe} + 3\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3$ is balanced. Left side has 4 Fe and 6 O; right side has 4 Fe and 6 O.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: ____$\text{Al}$ + ____$\text{Br}_2$ → ____$\text{AlBr}_3$
questions.explanation: $2\text{Al} + 3\text{Br}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{AlBr}_3$ is balanced. Left side has 2 Al and 6 Br; right side has 2 Al and 6 Br.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: ____$\text{Mg}$ + ____$\text{HCl}$ → ____$\text{MgCl}_2$ + ____$\text{H}_2$
questions.explanation: $\text{Mg} + 2\text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{MgCl}_2 + \text{H}_2$ is balanced. Left side has 1 Mg, 2 H, 2 Cl; right side has 1 Mg, 2 Cl, and H₂ (2 H).

questions.question: Fill in the blank: ____$\text{KClO}_3$ → ____$\text{KCl}$ + ____$\text{O}_2$
questions.explanation: $2\text{KClO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{KCl} + 3\text{O}_2$ is balanced. Left side has 2 K, 2 Cl, 6 O; right side has 2 K, 2 Cl, and 3 O₂ (6 O).

questions.question: Fill in the blank: ____$\text{BaCl}_2$ + ____$\text{Na}_2\text{SO}_4$ → ____$\text{BaSO}_4$ + ____$\text{NaCl}$
questions.explanation: $\text{BaCl}_2 + \text{Na}_2\text{SO}_4 \rightarrow \text{BaSO}_4 + 2\text{NaCl}$ is balanced. Left side has 1 Ba, 2 Cl, 2 Na, 1 SO₄; right side has 1 Ba, 1 SO₄, 2 Na, 2 Cl.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered balancing a wide variety of chemical equations! ⚖️🧪
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review equations with decomposition reactions and combustion.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on counting atoms carefully on both sides.
scoreMessages.0: Please review the Law of Conservation of Mass and practice balancing simple equations first.
